name: Aged HSCs were refractory to bloodborne rejuvenation
summary: Aged hematopoietic stem cells did not regain youthful function after bloodborne systemic rejuvenation interventions in mice.
statement: Aged hematopoietic stem cells remained functionally refractory after bloodborne systemic rejuvenation interventions, showing that circulating-factor modulation is not uniformly rejuvenating across aged stem-cell compartments.
population_or_model: Aged mouse hematopoietic stem cells and functional assays
quantitative_note: This finding is staged as boundary evidence against broad tissue-general claims.
caveats: The result is animal evidence and tissue-compartment specific.
It does not rule out effects in other tissues or with more targeted factors.
It does limit claims that young systemic environments automatically rejuvenate all aged cell types.
